message:
test(fnp-python): serialize AttrGuard poisoning via process-wide mutex

Four test sites swap shared Python globals (numpy.frompyfunc,
linalg.pinv, linalg.eigvals, ma.minimum_fill_value) with bomb/poison
sentinels via AttrGuard. Prior reports (bead 4jlw) showed intermittent
parallel-test failures when a sibling thread read the polluted state
mid-test. This commit serializes every AttrGuard construction behind a
static POISON_MUTEX held for the guard's lifetime, so only one poisoning
test runs at any time. The mutex poisoning path recovers via
into_inner() so a panicking test still releases the lock.

Verified: 5/5 parallel runs of `cargo test -p fnp-python --lib` pass
395/395 with no flakes. The three previously-intermittent tests
(count_nonzero_*, frompyfunc_live_callable_*_reduce_scalar_where*) are
stable under parallel execution.

Resolves franken_numpy-4jlw.

br-franken_numpy-4jlw
